sector_focus.secondary: telecom + AI — international, strongest resume match
sector_focus.tertiary: Technical Product Manager — any industry, Chicago area priority
sector_focus.tpm_chicago_note: TPM roles of any type in Chicago/hybrid score on par with primary. Deep delivery background is directly applicable regardless of industry.
sector_focus.note: Deep telecom background makes telecom AI roles internationally equivalent to Chicago primary. Float these to top.

geo_priority.scope: all TPM roles (any industry) + agentic AI + IT/technology leadership

geo_priority.role_scope: Principal, Director, VP, or equivalent — telecom + AI only
geo_priority.language_edge: German preferred for Deutsche Telekom and DACH region

network.note: If opportunity company matches a company in network_companies.json, flag as warm lead and boost score

career_focus.narrative: Technology leader and hands-on builder. Telecom, satellite, and IoT background. Production agentic AI systems. Deep cross-cultural experience across Brazil, UAE, and Latin America. Strong judgment on AI solution design and delivery.
career_focus.cos_self_improve: CoS should surface new search sources or APIs as recommendations when better signal is found. Robert decides whether to activate.

german.watch_for: emerging language learning tools, AI tutors, conversation groups, new language learning APIs

curator.scout_for: emerging AI governance, telecom AI, MENA geopolitics, USD hegemony, agentic AI platforms

build_discipline._note: staleness_days: how long a spec_ready/in_build item can go without progress before CoS flags it blocked. recently_done_grace_days: how long a done item stays on the Queue board before dropping to Build Log only. Currently both 3 — independent values, can diverge if useful.

github_sanity_check.frequency: after significant build sessions or when queue drift is suspected
what_to_check: Confirm what is actually committed to the repo vs. what specs say was shipped
Check for drift between _working/ROADMAP.md status and actual commit history
Flag any specs marked done in the queue that have no corresponding commit
Flag any commits that did not produce a queue update
github_sanity_check.output: Short briefing note to Robert — not build queue items. Format: '3 items confirmed, 1 drift found: X was marked done but no commit found.'
github_sanity_check.note: Audit function, not a build function. Surface findings only.
